source:
# Create Postcode Custom Classification in Purview

Registers a custom classification type `Postcode` and a regex-based classification rule
in Microsoft Purview so the anonymisation engine can detect and redact postcode columns
to their first block (e.g. `SW1W 9SP` → `SW1W`).

Run this notebook **once per environment** (dev / preprod / prod) before scanning assets.
It is idempotent — re-running when the classification already exists is safe.

import json
import requests
from notebookutils import mssparkutils
from odw.core.anonymisation.engine import (
    _get_access_token,
    _kv_secret_via_linked_service,
    DEFAULT_PURVIEW_NAME,
    DEFAULT_TENANT_ID,
    _DEFAULT_PURVIEW_CLIENT_ID_SECRET,
    _DEFAULT_PURVIEW_CLIENT_SECRET_SECRET,
)

## Configuration

Override `PURVIEW_NAME` if running against preprod or prod.

PURVIEW_NAME = DEFAULT_PURVIEW_NAME  # e.g. 'pins-pview'
TENANT_ID    = DEFAULT_TENANT_ID

CLIENT_ID     = mssparkutils.credentials.getSecretWithLS('ls_kv', _DEFAULT_PURVIEW_CLIENT_ID_SECRET)
CLIENT_SECRET = mssparkutils.credentials.getSecretWithLS('ls_kv', _DEFAULT_PURVIEW_CLIENT_SECRET_SECRET)

ATLAS_BASE = f'https://{PURVIEW_NAME}.catalog.purview.azure.com/api/atlas/v2'
SCAN_BASE  = f'https://{PURVIEW_NAME}.scan.purview.azure.com'

CLASSIFICATION_NAME = 'UK Postcode'
RULE_NAME           = 'UK Postcode'  # no spaces — Purview rule names must be URL-safe

# Sanitise rule name: replace spaces with hyphens to avoid 400 from URL encoding
RULE_NAME = RULE_NAME.replace(' ', '-')

# Scan API version — the scan endpoint requires 2022-07-01-preview, not 2023-09-01
SCAN_API_VERSION = '2022-07-01-preview'

# UK postcode regex — matches both full (SW1W 9SP) and partial (SW1W) formats
UK_POSTCODE_REGEX = r'^[A-Z]{1,2}[0-9][0-9A-Z]?(\s+[0-9][A-Z]{2})?$'

print(f'Purview: {PURVIEW_NAME}')
print(f'Classification: {CLASSIFICATION_NAME}')
print(f'Rule: {RULE_NAME}')

## Step 1 — Register the classification type (Atlas API)

Creates the `Postcode` classification type. If it already exists the API returns 200 with no changes.

## Step 2 — Create a classification rule (Scan API)

Registers a regex-based scanning rule so Purview auto-tags columns whose values match
the UK postcode pattern with the `Postcode` classification.

Uses `PUT` so it is safe to re-run — it will update an existing rule with the same name.

## Step 3 — Verify

Confirms both the classification type and the rule are visible in Purview.

# Verify classification type exists
type_url  = f'{ATLAS_BASE}/types/classificationdef/name/{CLASSIFICATION_NAME}'
type_resp = requests.get(type_url, headers=headers, timeout=60)
type_resp.raise_for_status()
print(f'Classification type confirmed: {type_resp.json().get("name")}')

# Verify classification rule exists
rule_url  = f'{SCAN_BASE}/classificationrules/{RULE_NAME}?api-version={SCAN_API_VERSION}'
rule_resp = requests.get(rule_url, headers=headers, timeout=60)
rule_resp.raise_for_status()
props = rule_resp.json().get('properties', {})
print(f'Classification rule confirmed: {rule_resp.json().get("name")}')
print(f'  classificationName : {props.get("classificationName")}')
print(f'  ruleStatus         : {props.get("ruleStatus")}')
print(f'  minimumPercentage  : {props.get("minimumPercentageMatch")}%')
print(f'  dataPattern        : {props.get("dataPatterns", [{}])[0].get("pattern")}')
print(f'  columnPattern      : {props.get("columnPatterns", [{}])[0].get("pattern")}')
